---
title: "Case study: calibrating an SIR epidemic model"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Case study: calibrating an SIR epidemic model}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>", eval = FALSE)
```

This is an end-to-end applied example: inferring the transmission dynamics of
an epidemic from noisy surveillance data, where the model is a *simulator*
(an SIR compartmental model) with no tractable likelihood.

## The problem

An SIR model tracks susceptible, infected, and recovered counts. Two
parameters govern the dynamics: the contact rate $\beta$ and the recovery
rate $\gamma$ (so $R_0 = \beta / \gamma$). We observe only noisy binomial
subsamples of the infected fraction at 10 time points — ex
